data.config.py:
"""Application configuration using pydantic-settings."""
from typing import Literal
from pydantic import Field, field_validator
from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict


class Settings(BaseSettings):
    """Application settings loaded from environment variables."""

    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(
        env_file=".env",
        env_file_encoding="utf-8",
        case_sensitive=False,
        extra="ignore"
    )

    # Application
    app_name: str = Field(default="DXG Backend", description="Application name")
    app_version: str = Field(default="0.1.0", description="Application version")
    environment: Literal["development", "staging", "production"] = Field(
        default="development",
        description="Runtime environment"
    )
    debug: bool = Field(default=False, description="Debug mode")

    # API Configuration
    api_v1_prefix: str = Field(default="/api/v1", description="API v1 route prefix")
    cors_origins: list[str] = Field(
        default=["http://localhost:3000", "http://localhost:5173"],
        description="Allowed CORS origins"
    )

    # Database
    database_url: str = Field(
        default="postgresql+asyncpg://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/dxg",
        description="PostgreSQL database URL (with async driver)"
    )
    db_echo: bool = Field(default=False, description="Echo SQL queries")
    db_pool_size: int = Field(default=10, ge=1, le=50, description="Database pool size")
    db_max_overflow: int = Field(default=20, ge=0, le=100, description="Max overflow connections")

    # Redis
    redis_url: str = Field(
        default="redis://localhost:6379/0",
        description="Redis connection URL"
    )
    redis_max_connections: int = Field(default=10, ge=1, le=100, description="Redis max connections")
    cache_ttl: int = Field(default=3600, ge=60, description="Default cache TTL in seconds")

    # ArangoDB Configuration (FlowMaster graph database)
    arango_url: str = Field(
        default="http://65.21.153.235:8529",
        description="ArangoDB server URL"
    )
    arango_database: str = Field(
        default="flowmaster",
        description="ArangoDB database name"
    )
    arango_username: str = Field(
        default="root",
        description="ArangoDB username"
    )
    arango_password: str = Field(
        default="flowmaster25",
        description="ArangoDB password"
    )

    # LLM Provider Selection
    llm_provider: Literal["openai", "anthropic"] = Field(
        default="anthropic",
        description="LLM provider to use (openai or anthropic)"
    )

    # OpenAI API
    openai_api_key: str = Field(
        default="",
        description="OpenAI API key"
    )
    openai_base_url: str = Field(
        default="",
        description="OpenAI API base URL (for Gemini or other OpenAI-compatible endpoints)"
    )
    openai_model: str = Field(
        default="gpt-4o",
        description="OpenAI model to use"
    )
    openai_max_tokens: int = Field(
        default=4096,
        ge=1,
        le=16384,
        description="Max tokens for OpenAI responses"
    )
    openai_timeout: int = Field(
        default=300,
        ge=30,
        le=600,
        description="OpenAI API timeout in seconds"
    )

    # Anthropic API
    anthropic_api_key: str = Field(
        default="",
        description="Anthropic API key"
    )
    anthropic_base_url: str = Field(
        default="",
        description="Anthropic API base URL (for proxy/LiteLLM)"
    )
    anthropic_model: str = Field(
        default="claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
        description="Anthropic Claude model to use"
    )
    anthropic_max_tokens: int = Field(
        default=4096,
        ge=1,
        le=16384,
        description="Max tokens for Anthropic responses"
    )
    anthropic_timeout: int = Field(
        default=300,
        ge=30,
        le=600,
        description="Anthropic API timeout in seconds"
    )

    # Generation Settings
    max_generation_retries: int = Field(default=3, ge=1, le=10, description="Max retry attempts")
    generation_timeout: int = Field(default=300, ge=60, le=600, description="Generation timeout")

    # Security
    secret_key: str = Field(
        default="change-me-in-production",
        min_length=32,
        description="Secret key for JWT and encryption"
    )

    # Logging
    log_level: Literal["DEBUG", "INFO", "WARNING", "ERROR", "CRITICAL"] = Field(
        default="INFO",
        description="Logging level"
    )

    @field_validator("openai_api_key")
    @classmethod
    def validate_openai_key(cls, v: str, info) -> str:
        """Validate OpenAI API key is set when using OpenAI provider."""
        provider = info.data.get("llm_provider", "anthropic")
        if provider == "openai" and info.data.get("environment") == "production" and not v:
            raise ValueError("openai_api_key must be set when using OpenAI provider in production")
        return v

    @field_validator("anthropic_api_key")
    @classmethod
    def validate_anthropic_key(cls, v: str, info) -> str:
        """Validate Anthropic API key is set when using Anthropic provider."""
        provider = info.data.get("llm_provider", "anthropic")
        if provider == "anthropic" and info.data.get("environment") == "production" and not v:
            raise ValueError("anthropic_api_key must be set when using Anthropic provider in production")
        return v

    @field_validator("secret_key")
    @classmethod
    def validate_secret_key(cls, v: str, info) -> str:
        """Validate secret key in production."""
        if info.data.get("environment") == "production" and v == "change-me-in-production":
            raise ValueError("secret_key must be changed in production")
        return v


# Global settings instance
settings = Settings()
data.openai_service.py:
"""OpenAI API integration service."""
import time
from typing import Optional, Any
from openai import AsyncOpenAI
from openai.types.chat import ChatCompletion

from src.config import settings


class OpenAIError(Exception):
    """Base exception for OpenAI service errors."""

    pass


class OpenAIAPIError(OpenAIError):
    """Exception for OpenAI API errors."""

    def __init__(self, message: str, status_code: Optional[int] = None):
        super().__init__(message)
        self.status_code = status_code


class OpenAITimeoutError(OpenAIError):
    """Exception for API timeout errors."""

    pass


class UsageStats:
    """Usage statistics for token tracking."""

    def __init__(self, input_tokens: int, output_tokens: int):
        self.input_tokens = input_tokens
        self.output_tokens = output_tokens


class OpenAIService:
    """
    Service for interacting with OpenAI-compatible APIs.

    Provides methods for generating UI definitions using OpenAI models
    (or compatible endpoints like Google Gemini) with proper error handling,
    retry logic, and metrics tracking.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        api_key: Optional[str] = None,
        base_url: Optional[str] = None,
        model: Optional[str] = None,
        max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
        timeout: Optional[int] = None,
    ):
        """
        Initialize OpenAI service.

        Args:
            api_key: OpenAI API key (defaults to settings)
            base_url: OpenAI API base URL (defaults to settings, for Gemini etc.)
            model: OpenAI model to use (defaults to settings)
            max_tokens: Maximum tokens for responses (defaults to settings)
            timeout: API timeout in seconds (defaults to settings)

        Raises:
            ValueError: If API key is not provided
        """
        self.api_key = api_key or settings.openai_api_key
        if not self.api_key:
            raise ValueError("OpenAI API key is required")

        self.base_url = base_url or settings.openai_base_url or None
        self.model = model or settings.openai_model
        self.max_tokens = max_tokens or settings.openai_max_tokens
        self.timeout = timeout or settings.openai_timeout

        # Initialize async client
        client_kwargs = {
            "api_key": self.api_key,
            "timeout": self.timeout,
        }
        if self.base_url:
            client_kwargs["base_url"] = self.base_url

        self.async_client = AsyncOpenAI(**client_kwargs)

    async def generate_ui_definition(
        self,
        prompt: str,
        system_prompt: Optional[str] = None,
        context: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
        temperature: Optional[float] = None,
        model: Optional[str] = None,
        max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    ) -> tuple[str, UsageStats, int, str, str]:
        """
        Generate UI definition using OpenAI.

        Args:
            prompt: User prompt describing the UI to generate
            system_prompt: Optional system prompt for context
            context: Optional additional context
            temperature: Sampling temperature (overrides default)
            model: Model to use (overrides default)
            max_tokens: Max tokens (overrides default)

        Returns:
            Tuple of (response_text, usage_stats, generation_time_ms, llm_request, llm_response)

        Raises:
            OpenAIAPIError: If API request fails
            OpenAITimeoutError: If request times out
            OpenAIError: For other OpenAI-related errors
        """
        start_time = time.time()

        try:
            # Build system prompt
            full_system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt(system_prompt, context)

            # Build messages for chat completion
            messages = [
                {"role": "system", "content": full_system_prompt},
                {"role": "user", "content": prompt}
            ]

            # Capture request for debugging
            import json
            llm_request = json.dumps({
                "model": model or self.model,
                "messages": messages,
                "max_tokens": max_tokens or self.max_tokens,
                "temperature": temperature if temperature is not None else 0.7,
            }, indent=2)

            # Make API call with overridable parameters
            completion: ChatCompletion = await self.async_client.chat.completions.create(
                model=model or self.model,
                messages=messages,
                max_tokens=max_tokens or self.max_tokens,
                temperature=temperature if temperature is not None else 0.7,
            )

            # Calculate duration
            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start_time) * 1000)

            # Extract response text
            response_text = completion.choices[0].message.content or ""

            # Capture response for debugging
            llm_response = json.dumps({
                "content": response_text,
                "usage": {
                    "prompt_tokens": completion.usage.prompt_tokens if completion.usage else 0,
                    "completion_tokens": completion.usage.completion_tokens if completion.usage else 0,
                },
                "model": completion.model,
                "finish_reason": completion.choices[0].finish_reason if completion.choices else None,
            }, indent=2)

            # Extract usage stats
            usage = UsageStats(
                input_tokens=completion.usage.prompt_tokens if completion.usage else 0,
                output_tokens=completion.usage.completion_tokens if completion.usage else 0,
            )

            return response_text, usage, duration_ms, llm_request, llm_response

        except Exception as e:
            # Classify and re-raise errors
            if "timeout" in str(e).lower():
                raise OpenAITimeoutError(f"OpenAI API request timed out: {e}") from e
            elif hasattr(e, "status_code"):
                raise OpenAIAPIError(
                    f"OpenAI API error: {e}",
                    status_code=getattr(e, "status_code", None)
                ) from e
            else:
                raise OpenAIError(f"Unexpected error calling OpenAI API: {e}") from e

    def _build_system_prompt(
        self,
        system_prompt: Optional[str],
        context: Optional[dict[str, Any]],
    ) -> str:
        """
        Build complete system prompt with context.

        Args:
            system_prompt: Base system prompt
            context: Additional context to include

        Returns:
            Complete system prompt
        """
        parts = []

        # Base system prompt
        if system_prompt:
            parts.append(system_prompt)
        else:
            parts.append(
                "You are a UI definition generator. Generate structured JSON "
                "definitions for UI components based on natural language descriptions. "
                "Always respond with valid JSON only, no additional text."
            )

        # Add context if provided
        if context:
            parts.append("\n\nAdditional Context:")
            for key, value in context.items():
                parts.append(f"- {key}: {value}")

        return "\n".join(parts)

    async def health_check(self) -> tuple[bool, Optional[str], Optional[float]]:
        """
        Check if OpenAI API is accessible.

        Returns:
            Tuple of (is_healthy, error_message, latency_ms)
        """
        start_time = time.time()

        try:
            # Make minimal API call
            await self.async_client.chat.completions.create(
                model=self.model,
                messages=[
                    {"role": "user", "content": "ping"}
                ],
                max_tokens=10,
            )

            latency_ms = (time.time() - start_time) * 1000
            return True, None, latency_ms

        except Exception as e:
            latency_ms = (time.time() - start_time) * 1000
            return False, str(e), latency_ms

    async def __aenter__(self):
        """Async context manager entry."""
        return self

    async def __aexit__(self, exc_type, exc_val, exc_tb):
        """Async context manager exit."""
        await self.async_client.close()
